mapping.a_element: the kappa-Minkowski / Lie-algebra non-commutative space-time whose coordinates obey a Lie-algebra commutation rule
mapping.b_element: the n-dimensional Lie algebra g specified by its structure constants

mapping.a_element: the star product that realizes the non-commutative coordinate algebra as an operation on ordinary (commuting) functions
mapping.b_element: the representation of the generators X_i as formal power series with coefficients in the Weyl algebra A_n (operators on commuting coordinates)

mapping.a_element: the kappa-Poincare coproduct that dictates how momenta combine (non-symmetrically) at interaction vertices
mapping.b_element: the Hopf-algebra / coderivation coproduct used in the third proof

mapping.a_element: the deformation controlled by the non-commutativity (1/kappa) scale organizing the expansion
mapping.b_element: the formal variable t in which the generators X_i = X_i^0 t are expanded as a power series

mapping.a_element: the non-symmetric, structure-dependent kernel the coproduct/star product imposes on momenta
mapping.b_element: the structure-constant combinatorial formula involving Bernoulli numbers and coth(x/2) (a BCH-type kernel)

brief_justification: Both rest on the same machinery: realizing an abstract Lie algebra as operators on ordinary commuting coordinates -- paper A's star product on kappa-Minkowski is the multiplicative face of paper B's Weyl-algebra realization of the Lie-algebra generators -- together with the dual Hopf-algebra coproduct (A's kappa-Poincare coproduct = B's coderivation/Hopf coproduct), the same structure-constant/BCH kernel (coth, Bernoulli numbers) encoding the non-commutativity in both.
